## [15:17 - 18:23]

let's say you decide to do a big mailing or all of a sudden you get a joint venture partner who mails his list or whatever, or your business just starts growing really fast, the merchant account, the merchant accounts freak out about that, because they're not accustomed to that. They're accustomed to seeing growth, but very slow growth. And, you know, and the reason they freak out, they're very risk-aversive, because basically what they're doing, they're giving you a loan for that amount of money, and they're the ones on the line for that. If you have $100,000 worth of charges that come through, well, they deposit that under granting your account, but they're on the line for it because these people have, like, up to 90 days or whatever it is to do a charge back, you know, or, you know, you could be a flake and not honor your refunds, and the bank is the one, you know, on the line for it. So they're basically issuing you that amount of credit every month. So when they see huge spikes in volume or when your business grows really fast, they freak out, and how they, what they usually do, they usually shut you down, they shut down your processing, and they also may freeze your business checking account so you can't get your money out.
